commentaries.author: Douay Rheims 1609
commentaries.content:
For Salomon had made an eminent place of brass, and had put it in the midst of the * Temple, having five cubits of length, & breadth, & three of height: & he stood upon it: & afterward kneeling over against all the multitude of Israel, and (c) his hands lifted up toward heaven,
(c) Lifting up hands, stretching forth arms, and the like external gestures do much help internal attention in prayer, and also stir up others to godly imitation: and therefore hath been much practiced by devout persons both in the old and new Testament.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
